item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I don't even remember why I put that crosslink in the first place; I guess I just wanted to establish some lore but did it with reckless abandon.

Still, I did myself a favor and omitted the last collapsible from this article. I still consider this to be my weakest article so far, but at least some people enjoy it in spite of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was initially a no-vote for the longest time due to the ending, but… I'm gonna give this an upvote cause of how well everything else was executed. Seriously, it was like reading a movie.

Also, I'm curious about the reasoning for the crosslink at the end, particularly concerning the "out-of-universe knowledge."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot of good here, but the pacing and issues of clinicality ultimately weaken it too much for me. Also, I know you have a reason for the crosslink but for the life of me I couldn't figure it out. -1, this could be made better but given the age it seems unlikely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? Because I, a pretty new user, wrote a tale expanding on an established character and a very notable old article, and nobody seemed to mind. And there are several other examples of stuff like this happening and being received positively. So I don't really see what you mean by the site being "two-faced".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s the product of the majority of the site being two-faced. People refuse to accept the changing or even expansion of old articles, unless it’s a “respected” user doing it, in which case it’s completely fine to completely derail a classic, famous article. Site’s harsh to new users.

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded: I know this is a late reply but yes, I'd like that.

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded: The files you have here total 2.73 MB in size, which can be a bit of a strain on mobile devices with limited data plans. Would you like assistance with resizing those?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find the name of your task force to be in bad taste.

There are some places where the term "corpses" (re: the pigs or sheep) could maybe be replaced with a more appropriate word. "Most of these corpses consisted of skulls…" would sound more correct to me if a word like "remains" was subbed in, as a skull is only part of a corpse. Instances where "The corpse appears to have died/have been killed recently" also seem gramatically sketchy, as this wording sounds as if the corpse, already dead, has been killed. Perhaps "the animal" could be used here?

"With individual eyewitness reports consisting of a young and attractive woman located within the forest" is a sentence fragment.

There are some good spooky images here, especially with the oddly behaving animals, but it does feel a bit over filled. I'd consider what imagery has a real place in the narrative, and what is just extra flavor, and maybe streamline a bit.

No vote at this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are quite a few issues here with pacing, wording, dialog and tone - all very small, but taken altogether weaken what has the trappings of a gripping and atmospheric article - more than I could reasonably address in a single post while confined to mobile.

It'd be a lot easier for me to discuss some of this via irc/discord - and I'd be open to collabing on a rewrite, if that is something you'd be interested in.

As far as larger issues I can point out real quick, I'll second mickeyten that the "missing persons" sections should probably use imperial - but more importantly, they don't really seem to contribute all that much. I'd either excise all but one, and try to work in some story details, or otherwise try to sneak in some form of subtle tells to story progression if you're to use multiple. Just having several snippets that repeat the same thing adds useless fluff.

My biggest issue though is the final collapsible. The reveal, and the linked SCP, just feels random and disconnected to everything that's been built up prior to that point. It was a huge letdown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still working my way through this one, but want to obnoxiously point out a nitpick before I forget - in your missing poster reproductions, you may want to use imperial measurements rather than metric (as well as maybe some more variation between the posters), since this takes place in Kentucky. (Unless I misunderstood, and these are Foundation records rather than missing posters.)

ETA: As said above, this is kinda a mishmash of tropes, but I dig it a lot for the execution and because I'm a sucker for this kind of stuff. Without going into the article's content, I'd personally love some expansion on what's been discussed above.

It could use a proofreading pass, though; I saw a lot of odd phrasing, inconsistency and tone that I don't think was intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please put anything related to the article in a collapsible.
I don't want anyone else to get spoiled.

SCP-053 is a crummy Series I article with no intrigue, and reading it doesn't make me care about its backstory

That's why I made this article actually. A majority of Series 1 SCPs are very underdeveloped and have gained a bad reputation for being poorly written and such. No one's bothered to make connections to old scips like this mostly because its a waste of time. I made this with the idea that a Series 1 scip like 053 could have great potential.

Now I could go on a tirade on how underdeveloped Series 1 scips are, and the community's reaction to SCPs that crosslink to Series 1 SCPs, but that's for another comment to ask me that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I usually don't crosslink to Series 1 scips without a good reason for it, maybe you could find out what the connection is? This goes a lot deeper than just a simple crosslink for crosslink's sake.

Sort of a spoiler
-Hide

Although the real reason relies a little bit on author headcanon and some out-of-universe knowledge on it, although I'm happy to see anyone's guess on what this is and their interpretation of it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AA: aradia event, y0u say?

feedback collapsed at author's request!
:3c

Doesn't suck me in, really! It's a mishmash of cool ideas that makes other SCPs cool but the final product is less than the sum of its parts. Exploration logs, and a stinger "new message" attachment at the end, feel like they were included less because the SCP benefitted from them, and more because they're just what you're "supposed" to put in a successful article.

I like when an article gives lore to another SCP object, but the reason it doesn't work for me here is 1) SCP-053 is a crummy Series I article with no intrigue, and reading it doesn't make me care about its backstory; and 2) nothing about SCP-4530 or its properties seems like a good explanation for why SCP-053 has its anomalous effect (or vice versa)1, so the twist wasn't foreshadowed well. It wasn't bad, per se, but it's less "!" and more "oh, okay."

Footnotes
1. Unless I missed something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm.

On one hand, this isn't a terribly original collection of ideas. Haunted forest, dead animals, spooky witch, weird stuff happens, thrown in connection to iconic Series I skip… all of this feels familiar, borderline cliched.

On the other hand, it's well written and developed.

I'll lean into a +1 for effort and execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm… I want to like this, but it feels a bit… disjointed? There are a lot of good ideas here, and I know this is gonna sound odd, but it feels like there's almost too many good ideas and not enough to pull them into one coherent SCP. I'm gonna reread it later and see if it's just my sleep addled brain not making the proper connections, but for now, no vote from me.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
"I'm afraid to close my eyes, I'm afraid to open them."

- Heather Donahue, The Blair Witch Project

"Midway upon the journey of our life, I found myself within a dark forest, for the straightforward path had been lost."

- Dante Alighieri, Inferno, Canto I

My second SCP. Inspired by movies like The VVitch, It Comes At Night, The Blair Witch Project, with some Scarlet Letter thrown in just for good measure. Just in time for Spooktober too!

Big thanks to YossiLeiner, Eskobar, MaliceAforethought, HumidHermit, FloppyPhoenix, and The Great Hippo for their feedback. These guys are coolio.

Credits: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Temporary_shelter_in_Pitts_wood_Inclosure_New_Forest_-_geograph.org.uk_-_82658.jpg
https://www.maxpixel.net/Spruce-Woods-Forest-Gloomy-Fog-815421
https://www.pexels.com/photo/black-lady-creepy-ghost-halloween-980262/
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Landscape-nature-forest-trees-2_(24218576162).jpg

The first three images have been edited by me.
